channel.title: US Top News and Analysis
channel.description: CNBC is the world leader in business news and real-time financial market coverage. Find fast, actionable information.

item.title: Oil rises as White House says Iran ceasefire halts 60-day war deadline
item.description: Under the 1973 War Powers Resolution, a U.S. president needs to withdraw troops 60 days after reporting their deployment to Congress

item.title: Apple CEO Tim Cook warns of extended memory crunch. 'We'll look at a range of options'
item.description: In Apple's quarterly earnings report, CEO Tim Cook warned of further memory constraints in the coming quarters.

item.title: China’s EV price war turns into AI arms race beyond cheaper cars
item.description: Chinese EV firms are rapidly rolling out in-car AI features to gain an edge, but as the price war drags on, those technologies are quickly becoming commoditized.

item.title: Apple revenue guidance tops estimates on booming iPhone, Mac demand
item.description: Apple reported 17% revenue growth, topping estimates, even as iPhone sales came up short.

item.title: Video shows Cole Allen casing hotel, storming checkpoint in alleged Trump assassination attempt
item.description: U.S. Attorney Jeanine Pirro said there is no evidence that a Secret Service shot during the incident was the victim of friendly fire.

item.title: Now is the time to rebalance your portfolio and snap up these bonds, UBS says
item.description: Government bond yields have crept higher, presenting a compelling entry point for investors, according to the bank.

item.title: Trump signs DHS funding bill, ending shutdown for most of agency, including TSA
item.description: Trump on Thursday signed legislation to fund the Department of Homeland Security after the House approved the bill earlier in the afternoon.

item.title: Japan Airlines begins humanoid robot trials at Tokyo's Haneda airport as labor shortages bite
item.description: Tokyo's Haneda Airport is beginning a trial of humanoid robots in airport ground services amid chronic labor challenges and a rapidly ageing workforce.

item.title: Australia and Japan markets climb, looking past Iran war escalation fears
item.description: This comes as investors took in strong earnings stateside, looking past weaker-than-expected economic data and threats of escalation in Iran.

item.title: OpenAI trial recap: Musk concludes testimony, lawyers spar over second witness
item.description: Elon Musk sued OpenAI, Sam Altman and Greg Brockman in 2024, claiming they reneged on their promise to keep the artificial intelligence lab a nonprofit.

item.title: Trump signs executive order expanding retirement account access for workers
item.description: The president's executive order expands retirement account access for workers and will be integrated with the Saver's Match.

item.title: House strips MAHA-hated pesticide provisions from farm bill
item.description: MAHA advocates said the language that was stripped could have provided liability protection for Bayer in lawsuits involving its Roundup weedkiller.

item.title: Jim Cramer says the market’s biggest winners all have one thing in common
item.description: CNBC's Jim Cramer said he only sees two sectors in the market these days — the data center stocks and everything else.

item.title: Market indicator turns bullish for Apple just before earnings
item.description: One measure of downside risk has fallen precipitously in the past five weeks.

item.title: Apple delivers a nearly perfect quarter, with a CEO change and an AI update ahead
item.description: In fact, it was the best March quarter in company history, and Apple stock jumped 4% in after-hours trading.

item.title: Intel's stock more than doubles in April for best month in chipmaker's 55 years on Nasdaq
item.description: Intel's market cap more than doubled in April, boosted by a blowout earnings report and optimism that the chipmaker is in the midst of a revival.

item.title: Reddit reports 69% jump in revenue, topping analyst estimates
item.description: Reddit reported first-quarter earnings and revenue that exceeded Wall Street expectations.

item.title: China’s self-driving truck leaders say AI breakthroughs won’t accelerate rollout — here’s why
item.description: Rapid advances in AI for coding and chatbots doesn't change the timeline for getting self-driving vehicles on the road, industry executives in China said.

item.title: Veeva Systems to join S&P 500 index, replacing Coterra Energy
item.description: Veeva's stock has been hit this year along with the rest of cloud software as investors fear the impact of AI.

item.title: FISA Section 702: Congress passes short-term surveillance program extension just before deadline
item.description: The controversial foreign surveillance program was due to expire by the end of Thursday.

item.title: Google cloud growth tops Microsoft and Amazon as all three beat estimates on AI demand
item.description: Amazon, Google and Microsoft all reported better-than-expected first-quarter cloud results, signaling an acceleration of AI demand

item.title: You don't have to wait for the TrumpIRA to start saving for retirement. Use these 3 tools to start saving now
item.description: How IRAs, annuities and mortgage refinancing can help you build retirement savings right now.

item.title: Inside the Fed: Powell vows he won't be a 'shadow chair,' but a Warsh clash will be tough to avoid
item.description: When the Fed gathers again, it will mark the first time a sitting and former chair conduct business together in nearly 80 years.

item.title: Friday's big stock stories: What’s likely to move the market in the next trading session
item.description: Stocks ended April's trading on a high note, with the S&P 500 closing above 7,200 for the first time and posting its strongly monthly performance since 2020.

item.title: Why Portland's empty store fronts are causing a budget crisis
item.description: Cities in the US are finally seeing easing in office and retail vacancies after years of a post-pandemic crunch in the commercial real estate space. However, for mid-sized cities like Portland Oregon the story is a little different. Empty store fronts, vacant office buildings, and a struggling downtown isn't just an eye sore, its having a real impact on the city's bottom line. CNBC's Diana Olick talks the city's plans to bring foot traffic back to its downtown.

item.title: Rivian renegotiates DOE loan down to $4.5 billion, adjusts capacity plans for Georgia plant
item.description: The DOE loan was previously set to support two phases of production for a total of 400,000 units annually.

item.title: Trump says 'I don't think about' another assassination attempt
item.description: Trump also said he was not inclined to wear a bulletproof vest, as some people have suggested after the arrest of a gunman over the weekend.

item.title: Apple's stock has gone nowhere for 6 months. Traders expect a wild ride Thursday night
item.description: Implied volatility in the stock suggests a 3.5% swing after earnings tonight, compared to an average move of just 1.8% following the last four quarterly reports.

item.title: AI boom: Big Tech capital expenditures now seen topping $1 trillion in 2027
item.description: The overall cost has been causing heads to spin, but analysts say they're seeing flowthrough from investments to revenue.

item.title: U.S. senators ban themselves from prediction markets trading
item.description: The move came after Democratic lawmakers asked the Commodity Futures Trading Commission to prohibit event contracts on elections, war and military actions.
